commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an `openclaw doctor` prompt-cache configuration warning collector for cache-ttl/model/heartbeat mismatches, wires it into doctor config flow, adds focused tests, and removes stale image provider casts.

PR surface: Source +678, Tests +642. Total +1320 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: Source-level yes: current main has cache-ttl runtime eligibility and retention behavior but no matching doctor advisory, while the PR adds the missing collector and tests. I did not run a live doctor repro because this review is read-only and the PR body already provides remote changed-gate proof.
commands.review_followup: The remaining action is maintainer review of advisory wording and provider cache assumptions, not a narrow automated repair.

target.title: fix(doctor): warn on stale prompt cache config
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds `openclaw doctor` warnings for cache-ttl configs whose selected chat model/provider cannot keep the prompt-cache window warm.
- Covers direct Anthropic, Anthropic-compatible providers, Bedrock Claude, Google prompt-cache models, OpenAI-family unsupported routes, aliases, auth-profile suffixes, subagent/channel routes, inherited agent defaults, fallback models, and heartbeat model/cadence mismatches.
- Uses already-loaded provider hooks plus bounded static provider knowledge so doctor does not introduce new config seams or trigger runtime plugin loading.
- Includes a separate small lint cleanup in `src/agents/tools/image-tool.ts`; current changed-gate lint was failing on stale casts from main.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

Requested by maintainer during beta cache-hit investigation after `/status` reported 0% cache hits with cache-ttl expectations.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `openclaw doctor` now warns when cache-ttl pruning is configured with a model/provider setup that will not maintain the expected prompt cache window.
- Real environment tested: Blacksmith Testbox through Crabbox on rebased commit `3e3c4e1b6c4` / `249aebb2c9e` over `origin/main` `d1c8f09b006`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider blacksmith-testbox --blacksmith-org openclaw --blacksmith-workflow .github/workflows/ci-check-testbox.yml --blacksmith-job check --blacksmith-ref main --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 240m --timing-json --shell -- "corepack pnpm check:changed"`.
- Evidence after fix: Crabbox/Testbox `tbx_01ksj9zx6d7q759zqxvc61cjzf`, Actions run https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26453450162, exit 0.
- Observed result after fix: changed gate completed with typecheck core, typecheck core tests, lint core, and guard checks passing.
- What was not tested: live provider cache-hit behavior against external model APIs.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: the local Vitest runner in this Codex worktree repeatedly hung; focused behavior was smoke-probed with direct `tsx` invocations and the full changed gate was run remotely.
- Before evidence: beta `/status` showed `Cache: 0% hit · 0 cached, 20k new` in a cache-ttl setup.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `timeout 15s node --import tsx -e "import('./src/commands/doctor/shared/prompt-cache-config-warnings.ts').then(()=>console.log('ok'))"`
- direct `node --import tsx` smoke probes for direct Anthropic clean config, custom `anthropic-messages` explicit-retention warnings, Bedrock Claude explicit-retention warnings, DeepInfra Anthropic static eligibility, and OpenRouter heartbeat mismatch warnings
- Crabbox/Testbox `corepack pnpm check:changed`: `tbx_01ksj9zx6d7q759zqxvc61cjzf`, exit 0

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Added focused doctor warning coverage for retention windows, default TTL/heartbeat behavior, OpenAI-family unsupported routes, aliases, auth-profile suffixes, legacy `cacheControlTtl`, configured provider APIs, default/fallback model resolution, per-agent overrides, subagent/channel routes, heartbeat mismatch behavior, plugin/static provider eligibility, and explicit-retention requirements for Google/custom Anthropic-compatible/Bedrock Claude routes.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- Prior changed-gate attempts caught TS import/fixture issues in the new tests and an unrelated current-main oxlint stale-cast failure; all were fixed before this PR was opened.

If no test was added, why not?

- Tests were added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Doctor can now emit advisory warnings for non-ideal prompt cache configs.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No config writes or migrations change. This is advisory-only.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No. Doctor uses loaded provider hooks plus static checks and avoids loading provider runtime plugins for this advisory.

What is the highest-risk area?

False positives on proxy/provider cache semantics.

How is that risk mitigated?

The advisory mirrors runtime cache-retention rules where possible, avoids broad plugin loading, uses explicit retention requirements only where runtime needs explicit cache config, and covers aliases/channel/subagent/inherited routes in tests.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Review doctor advisory wording and provider eligibility assumptions.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

CI on the draft PR.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Local autoreview attempts found edge cases that were folded into the patch; the final local autoreview run could not complete because it launched the known-hanging local Vitest path.
target.author: vincentkoc
